     1	package server
     2	
     3	import (
     4	        "context"
     5	        _ "embed"
     6	
     7	        "github.com/rs/xid"
     8	
     9	        "github.com/netbirdio/netbird/management/proto"
    10	        "github.com/netbirdio/netbird/management/server/permissions/modules"
    11	        "github.com/netbirdio/netbird/management/server/permissions/operations"
    12	        "github.com/netbirdio/netbird/management/server/store"
    13	        "github.com/netbirdio/netbird/management/server/types"
    14	
    15	        "github.com/netbirdio/netbird/management/server/activity"
    16	        "github.com/netbirdio/netbird/management/server/posture"
    17	        "github.com/netbirdio/netbird/management/server/status"
    18	)
    19	
    20	// GetPolicy from the store
    21	func (am *DefaultAccountManager) GetPolicy(ctx context.Context, accountID, policyID, userID string) (*types.Policy, error) {
    22	        allowed, err := am.permissionsManager.ValidateUserPermissions(ctx, accountID, userID, modules.Policies, operations.Read)
    23	        if err != nil {
    24	                return nil, status.NewPermissionValidationError(err)
    25	        }
    26	        if !allowed {
    27	                return nil, status.NewPermissionDeniedError()
    28	        }
    29	
    30	        return am.Store.GetPolicyByID(ctx, store.LockingStrengthShare, accountID, policyID)
    31	}
    32	
    33	// SavePolicy in the store
    34	func (am *DefaultAccountManager) SavePolicy(ctx context.Context, accountID, userID string, policy *types.Policy, create bool) (*types.Policy, error) {
    35	        unlock := am.Store.AcquireWriteLockByUID(ctx, accountID)
    36	        defer unlock()
    37	
    38	        operation := operations.Create
    39	        if !create {
    40	                operation = operations.Update
    41	        }
    42	        allowed, err := am.permissionsManager.ValidateUserPermissions(ctx, accountID, userID, modules.Policies, operation)
    43	        if err != nil {
    44	                return nil, status.NewPermissionValidationError(err)
    45	        }
    46	        if !allowed {
    47	                return nil, status.NewPermissionDeniedError()
    48	        }
    49	
    50	        var isUpdate = policy.ID != ""
    51	        var updateAccountPeers bool
    52	        var action = activity.PolicyAdded
    53	
    54	        err = am.Store.ExecuteInTransaction(ctx, func(transaction store.Store) error {
    55	                if err = validatePolicy(ctx, transaction, accountID, policy); err != nil {
    56	                        return err
    57	                }
    58	
    59	                updateAccountPeers, err = arePolicyChangesAffectPeers(ctx, transaction, accountID, policy, isUpdate)
    60	                if err != nil {
    61	                        return err
    62	                }
    63	
    64	                if err = transaction.IncrementNetworkSerial(ctx, store.LockingStrengthUpdate, accountID); err != nil {
    65	                        return err
    66	                }
    67	
    68	                saveFunc := transaction.CreatePolicy
    69	                if isUpdate {
    70	                        action = activity.PolicyUpdated
    71	                        saveFunc = transaction.SavePolicy
    72	                }
    73	
    74	                return saveFunc(ctx, store.LockingStrengthUpdate, policy)
    75	        })
    76	        if err != nil {
    77	                return nil, err
    78	        }
    79	
    80	        am.StoreEvent(ctx, userID, policy.ID, accountID, action, policy.EventMeta())
    81	
    82	        if updateAccountPeers {
    83	                am.UpdateAccountPeers(ctx, accountID)
    84	        }
    85	
    86	        return policy, nil
    87	}
    88	
    89	// DeletePolicy from the store
    90	func (am *DefaultAccountManager) DeletePolicy(ctx context.Context, accountID, policyID, userID string) error {
    91	        unlock := am.Store.AcquireWriteLockByUID(ctx, accountID)
    92	        defer unlock()
    93	
    94	        allowed, err := am.permissionsManager.ValidateUserPermissions(ctx, accountID, userID, modules.Policies, operations.Delete)
    95	        if err != nil {
    96	                return status.NewPermissionValidationError(err)
    97	        }
    98	        if !allowed {
    99	                return status.NewPermissionDeniedError()
   100	        }
   101	
   102	        var policy *types.Policy
   103	        var updateAccountPeers bool
   104	
   105	        err = am.Store.ExecuteInTransaction(ctx, func(transaction store.Store) error {
   106	                policy, err = transaction.GetPolicyByID(ctx, store.LockingStrengthUpdate, accountID, policyID)
   107	                if err != nil {
   108	                        return err
   109	                }
   110	
   111	                updateAccountPeers, err = arePolicyChangesAffectPeers(ctx, transaction, accountID, policy, false)
   112	                if err != nil {
   113	                        return err
   114	                }
   115	
   116	                if err = transaction.IncrementNetworkSerial(ctx, store.LockingStrengthUpdate, accountID); err != nil {
   117	                        return err
   118	                }
   119	
   120	                return transaction.DeletePolicy(ctx, store.LockingStrengthUpdate, accountID, policyID)
   121	        })
   122	        if err != nil {
   123	                return err
   124	        }
   125	
   126	        am.StoreEvent(ctx, userID, policyID, accountID, activity.PolicyRemoved, policy.EventMeta())
   127	
   128	        if updateAccountPeers {
   129	                am.UpdateAccountPeers(ctx, accountID)
   130	        }
   131	
   132	        return nil
   133	}
   134	
   135	// ListPolicies from the store.
   136	func (am *DefaultAccountManager) ListPolicies(ctx context.Context, accountID, userID string) ([]*types.Policy, error) {
   137	        allowed, err := am.permissionsManager.ValidateUserPermissions(ctx, accountID, userID, modules.Policies, operations.Read)
   138	        if err != nil {
   139	                return nil, status.NewPermissionValidationError(err)
   140	        }
   141	        if !allowed {
   142	                return nil, status.NewPermissionDeniedError()
   143	        }
   144	
   145	        return am.Store.GetAccountPolicies(ctx, store.LockingStrengthShare, accountID)
   146	}
   147	
   148	// arePolicyChangesAffectPeers checks if changes to a policy will affect any associated peers.
   149	func arePolicyChangesAffectPeers(ctx context.Context, transaction store.Store, accountID string, policy *types.Policy, isUpdate bool) (bool, error) {
   150	        if isUpdate {
   151	                existingPolicy, err := transaction.GetPolicyByID(ctx, store.LockingStrengthShare, accountID, policy.ID)
   152	                if err != nil {
   153	                        return false, err
   154	                }
   155	
   156	                if !policy.Enabled && !existingPolicy.Enabled {
   157	                        return false, nil
   158	                }
   159	
   160	                hasPeers, err := anyGroupHasPeersOrResources(ctx, transaction, policy.AccountID, existingPolicy.RuleGroups())
   161	                if err != nil {
   162	                        return false, err
   163	                }
   164	
   165	                if hasPeers {
   166	                        return true, nil
   167	                }
   168	        }
   169	
   170	        return anyGroupHasPeersOrResources(ctx, transaction, policy.AccountID, policy.RuleGroups())
   171	}
   172	
   173	// validatePolicy validates the policy and its rules.
   174	func validatePolicy(ctx context.Context, transaction store.Store, accountID string, policy *types.Policy) error {
   175	        if policy.ID != "" {
   176	                _, err := transaction.GetPolicyByID(ctx, store.LockingStrengthShare, accountID, policy.ID)
   177	                if err != nil {
   178	                        return err
   179	                }
   180	        } else {
   181	                policy.ID = xid.New().String()
   182	                policy.AccountID = accountID
   183	        }
   184	
   185	        groups, err := transaction.GetGroupsByIDs(ctx, store.LockingStrengthShare, accountID, policy.RuleGroups())
   186	        if err != nil {
   187	                return err
   188	        }
   189	
   190	        postureChecks, err := transaction.GetPostureChecksByIDs(ctx, store.LockingStrengthShare, accountID, policy.SourcePostureChecks)
   191	        if err != nil {
   192	                return err
   193	        }
   194	
   195	        for i, rule := range policy.Rules {
   196	                ruleCopy := rule.Copy()
   197	                if ruleCopy.ID == "" {
   198	                        ruleCopy.ID = policy.ID // TODO: when policy can contain multiple rules, need refactor
   199	                        ruleCopy.PolicyID = policy.ID
   200	                }
   201	
   202	                ruleCopy.Sources = getValidGroupIDs(groups, ruleCopy.Sources)
   203	                ruleCopy.Destinations = getValidGroupIDs(groups, ruleCopy.Destinations)
   204	                policy.Rules[i] = ruleCopy
   205	        }
   206	
   207	        if policy.SourcePostureChecks != nil {
   208	                policy.SourcePostureChecks = getValidPostureCheckIDs(postureChecks, policy.SourcePostureChecks)
   209	        }
   210	
   211	        return nil
   212	}
   213	
   214	// getValidPostureCheckIDs filters and returns only the valid posture check IDs from the provided list.
   215	func getValidPostureCheckIDs(postureChecks map[string]*posture.Checks, postureChecksIds []string) []string {
   216	        validIDs := make([]string, 0, len(postureChecksIds))
   217	        for _, id := range postureChecksIds {
   218	                if _, exists := postureChecks[id]; exists {
   219	                        validIDs = append(validIDs, id)
   220	                }
   221	        }
   222	
   223	        return validIDs
   224	}
   225	
   226	// getValidGroupIDs filters and returns only the valid group IDs from the provided list.
   227	func getValidGroupIDs(groups map[string]*types.Group, groupIDs []string) []string {
   228	        validIDs := make([]string, 0, len(groupIDs))
   229	        for _, id := range groupIDs {
   230	                if _, exists := groups[id]; exists {
   231	                        validIDs = append(validIDs, id)
   232	                }
   233	        }
   234	
   235	        return validIDs
   236	}
   237	
   238	// toProtocolFirewallRules converts the firewall rules to the protocol firewall rules.
   239	func toProtocolFirewallRules(rules []*types.FirewallRule) []*proto.FirewallRule {
   240	        result := make([]*proto.FirewallRule, len(rules))
   241	        for i := range rules {
   242	                rule := rules[i]
   243	
   244	                fwRule := &proto.FirewallRule{
   245	                        PolicyID:  []byte(rule.PolicyID),
   246	                        PeerIP:    rule.PeerIP,
   247	                        Direction: getProtoDirection(rule.Direction),
   248	                        Action:    getProtoAction(rule.Action),
   249	                        Protocol:  getProtoProtocol(rule.Protocol),
   250	                        Port:      rule.Port,
   251	                }
   252	
   253	                if shouldUsePortRange(fwRule) {
   254	                        fwRule.PortInfo = rule.PortRange.ToProto()
   255	                }
   256	
   257	                result[i] = fwRule
   258	        }
   259	        return result
   260	}
   261	
   262	func shouldUsePortRange(rule *proto.FirewallRule) bool {
   263	        return rule.Port == "" && (rule.Protocol == proto.RuleProtocol_UDP || rule.Protocol == proto.RuleProtocol_TCP)
   264	}
   265

    57	func cacheEntryExpiration() time.Duration {
    58	        r := rand.Intn(int(nbcache.DefaultIDPCacheExpirationMax.Milliseconds()-nbcache.DefaultIDPCacheExpirationMin.Milliseconds())) + int(nbcache.DefaultIDPCacheExpirationMin.Milliseconds())
    59	        return time.Duration(r) * time.Millisecond
    60	}

    62	type DefaultAccountManager struct {
    63	        Store store.Store
    64	        // cacheMux and cacheLoading helps to make sure that only a single cache reload runs at a time per accountID
    65	        cacheMux sync.Mutex
    66	        // cacheLoading keeps the accountIDs that are currently reloading. The accountID has to be removed once cache has been reloaded
    67	        cacheLoading         map[string]chan struct{}
    68	        peersUpdateManager   *PeersUpdateManager
    69	        idpManager           idp.Manager
    70	        cacheManager         *nbcache.AccountUserDataCache
    71	        externalCacheManager nbcache.UserDataCache

    79	        settingsManager settings.Manager
    80	
    81	        // singleAccountMode indicates whether the instance has a single account.
    82	        // If true, then every new user will end up under the same account.
    83	        // This value will be set to false if management service has more than one account.
    84	        singleAccountMode bool
    85	        // singleAccountModeDomain is a domain to use in singleAccountMode setup
    86	        singleAccountModeDomain string
    87	        // dnsDomain is used for peer resolution. This is appended to the peer's name
    88	        dnsDomain       string
    89	        peerLoginExpiry Scheduler
    90

    92

    97

   105	
   106	// getJWTGroupsChanges calculates the changes needed to sync a user's JWT groups.
   107	// Returns a bool indicating if there are changes in the JWT group membership, the updated user AutoGroups,
   108	// newly groups to create and an error if any occurred.
   109	func (am *DefaultAccountManager) getJWTGroupsChanges(user *types.User, groups []*types.Group, groupNames []string) (bool, []string, []*types.Group, error) {
   110	        existedGroupsByName := make(map[string]*types.Group)
   111	        for _, group := range groups {
   112	                existedGroupsByName[group.Name] = group
   113	        }
   114	
   115	        newUserAutoGroups, jwtGroupsMap := separateGroups(user.AutoGroups, groups)
   116	
   117	        groupsToAdd := util.Difference(groupNames, maps.Keys(jwtGroupsMap))
   118	        groupsToRemove := util.Difference(maps.Keys(jwtGroupsMap), groupNames)
   119	
   120	        // If no groups are added or removed, we should not sync account
   121	        if len(groupsToAdd) == 0 && len(groupsToRemove) == 0 {
   122	                return false, nil, nil, nil
   123	        }
   124	
   125	        newGroupsToCreate := make([]*types.Group, 0)
   126	
   127	        var modified bool
   128	        for _, name := range groupsToAdd {
   129	                group, exists := existedGroupsByName[name]
   130	                if !exists {
   131	                        group = &types.Group{
   132	                                ID:        xid.New().String(),
   133	                                AccountID: user.AccountID,
   134	                                Name:      name,
   135	                                Issued:    types.GroupIssuedJWT,
   136	                        }
   137	                        newGroupsToCreate = append(newGroupsToCreate, group)
   138	                }
   139	                if group.Issued == types.GroupIssuedJWT {
   140	                        newUserAutoGroups = append(newUserAutoGroups, group.ID)
   141	                        modified = true
   142	                }
   143	        }
   144	
   145	        for name, id := range jwtGroupsMap {
   146	                if !slices.Contains(groupsToRemove, name) {
   147	                        newUserAutoGroups = append(newUserAutoGroups, id)
   148	                        continue
   149	                }
   150	                modified = true
   151	        }
   152	
   153	        return modified, newUserAutoGroups, newGroupsToCreate, nil
   154	}
   155	
   156	// BuildManager creates a new DefaultAccountManager with a provided Store
   157	func BuildManager(
   158	        ctx context.Context,
   159	        store store.Store,
   160	        peersUpdateManager *PeersUpdateManager,
   161	        idpManager idp.Manager,
   162	        singleAccountModeDomain string,
   163	        dnsDomain string,
   164	        eventStore activity.Store,
   165	        geo geolocation.Geolocation,
   166	        userDeleteFromIDPEnabled bool,
   167	        integratedPeerValidator integrated_validator.IntegratedValidator,
   168	        metrics telemetry.AppMetrics,
   169	        proxyController port_forwarding.Controller,
   170	        settingsManager settings.Manager,
   171	        permissionsManager permissions.Manager,
   172	) (*DefaultAccountManager, error) {
   173	        start := time.Now()
   174	        defer func() {
   175	                log.WithContext(ctx).Debugf("took %v to instantiate account manager", time.Since(start))
   176	        }()
   177	
   178	        am := &DefaultAccountManager{
   179	                Store:                    store,
   180	                geo:                      geo,
   181	                peersUpdateManager:       peersUpdateManager,
   182	                idpManager:               idpManager,
   183	                ctx:                      context.Background(),
   184	                cacheMux:                 sync.Mutex{},
   185	                cacheLoading:             map[string]chan struct{}{},
   186	                dnsDomain:                dnsDomain,
   187	                eventStore:               eventStore,
   188	                peerLoginExpiry:          NewDefaultScheduler(),
   189	                peerInactivityExpiry:     NewDefaultScheduler(),
   190	                userDeleteFromIDPEnabled: userDeleteFromIDPEnabled,
   191	                integratedPeerValidator:  integratedPeerValidator,
   192	                metrics:                  metrics,
   193	                requestBuffer:            NewAccountRequestBuffer(ctx, store),
   194	                proxyController:          proxyController,
   195	                settingsManager:          settingsManager,
   196	                permissionsManager:       permissionsManager,
   197	        }
   198	
   199	        am.startWarmup(ctx)
   200	
   201	        accountsCounter, err := store.GetAccountsCounter(ctx)
   202	        if err != nil {
   203	                log.WithContext(ctx).Error(err)
   204	        }
   205	
   206	        // enable single account mode only if configured by user and number of existing accounts is not grater than 1
   207	        am.singleAccountMode = singleAccountModeDomain != "" && accountsCounter <= 1
   208	        if am.singleAccountMode {
   209	                if !isDomainValid(singleAccountModeDomain) {
   210	                        return nil, status.Errorf(status.InvalidArgument, "invalid domain \"%s\" provided for a single account mode. Please review your input for --single-account-mode-domain", singleAccountModeDomain)
   211	                }
   212	                am.singleAccountModeDomain = singleAccountModeDomain
   213	                log.WithContext(ctx).Infof("single account mode enabled, accounts number %d", accountsCounter)
   214	        } else {
   215	                log.WithContext(ctx).Infof("single account mode disabled, accounts number %d", accountsCounter)
   216	        }
   217	
   218	        cacheStore, err := nbcache.NewStore(ctx, nbcache.DefaultIDPCacheExpirationMax, nbcache.DefaultIDPCacheCleanupInterval)
   219	        if err != nil {
   220	                return nil, fmt.Errorf("getting cache store: %s", err)
   221	        }
   222	        am.externalCacheManager = nbcache.NewUserDataCache(cacheStore)
   223	        am.cacheManager = nbcache.NewAccountUserDataCache(am.loadAccount, cacheStore)
   224	
   225	        if !isNil(am.idpManager) {
   226	                go func() {
   227	                        err := am.warmupIDPCache(ctx, cacheStore)
   228	                        if err != nil {
   229	                                log.WithContext(ctx).Warnf("failed warming up cache due to error: %v", err)
   230	                                // todo retry?
   231	                                return
   232	                        }
   233	                }()
   234	        }
   235	
   236	        am.integratedPeerValidator.SetPeerInvalidationListener(func(accountID string) {
   237	                am.onPeersInvalidated(ctx, accountID)
   238	        })
   239	
   240	        return am, nil
   241	}
   242	
   243	func (am *DefaultAccountManager) startWarmup(ctx context.Context) {
   244	        var initialInterval int64
   245	        intervalStr := os.Getenv("NB_PEER_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS")
   246	        interval, err := strconv.Atoi(intervalStr)
   247	        if err != nil {
   248	                initialInterval = 1
   249	                log.WithContext(ctx).Warnf("failed to parse peer update interval, using default value %dms: %v", initialInterval, err)
   250	        } else {
   251	                initialInterval = int64(interval) * 10
   252	                go func() {
   253	                        startupPeriodStr := os.Getenv("NB_PEER_UPDATE_STARTUP_PERIOD_S")
   254	                        startupPeriod, err := strconv.Atoi(startupPeriodStr)
   255	                        if err != nil {
   256	                                startupPeriod = 1
   257	                                log.WithContext(ctx).Warnf("failed to parse peer update startup period, using default value %ds: %v", startupPeriod, err)
   258	                        }
   259	                        time.Sleep(time.Duration(startupPeriod) * time.Second)
   260	                        am.updateAccountPeersBufferInterval.Store(int64(time.Duration(interval) * time.Millisecond))
   261	                        log.WithContext(ctx).Infof("set peer update buffer interval to %dms", interval)
   262	                }()
   263	        }
   264	        am.updateAccountPeersBufferInterval.Store(initialInterval)
   265	        log.WithContext(ctx).Infof("set peer update buffer interval to %dms", initialInterval)
   266	
   267	}
   268	
   269	func (am *DefaultAccountManager) GetExternalCacheManager() account.ExternalCacheManager {
   270	        return am.externalCacheManager
   271	}
   272	
   273	func (am *DefaultAccountManager) GetIdpManager() idp.Manager {
   274	        return am.idpManager
   275	}
   276	
   277	// UpdateAccountSettings updates Account settings.
   278	// Only users with role UserRoleAdmin can update the account.
   279	// User that performs the update has to belong to the account.
   280	// Returns an updated Settings
   281	func (am *DefaultAccountManager) UpdateAccountSettings(ctx context.Context, accountID, userID string, newSettings *types.Settings) (*types.Settings, error) {
   282	        unlock := am.Store.AcquireWriteLockByUID(ctx, accountID)
   283	        defer unlock()
   284	
   285	        allowed, err := am.permissionsManager.ValidateUserPermissions(ctx, accountID, userID, modules.Settings, operations.Update)
   286	        if err != nil {
   287	                return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to validate user permissions: %w", err)
   288	        }
   289	
   290	        if !allowed {
   291	                return nil, status.NewPermissionDeniedError()
   292	        }
   293	
   294	        var oldSettings *types.Settings
   295	        var updateAccountPeers bool
   296	        var groupChangesAffectPeers bool
   297	
   298	        err = am.Store.ExecuteInTransaction(ctx, func(transaction store.Store) error {
   299	                var groupsUpdated bool
   300	
   301	                oldSettings, err = transaction.GetAccountSettings(ctx, store.LockingStrengthUpdate, accountID)
   302	                if err != nil {
   303	                        return err
   304	                }
   305	
   306	                if err = am.validateSettingsUpdate(ctx, transaction, newSettings, oldSettings, userID, accountID); err != nil {
   307	                        return err
   308	                }
   309	
   310	                if oldSettings.RoutingPeerDNSResolutionEnabled != newSettings.RoutingPeerDNSResolutionEnabled ||
   311	                        oldSettings.LazyConnectionEnabled != newSettings.LazyConnectionEnabled ||
   312	                        oldSettings.DNSDomain != newSettings.DNSDomain {
   313	                        updateAccountPeers = true
   314	                }
   315	
   316	                if oldSettings.GroupsPropagationEnabled != newSettings.GroupsPropagationEnabled && newSettings.GroupsPropagationEnabled {
   317	                        groupsUpdated, groupChangesAffectPeers, err = propagateUserGroupMemberships(ctx, transaction, accountID)
   318	                        if err != nil {
   319	                                return err
   320	                        }
   321	                }
   322	
   323	                if updateAccountPeers || groupsUpdated {
   324	                        if err = transaction.IncrementNetworkSerial(ctx, store.LockingStrengthUpdate, accountID); err != nil {
   325	                                return err
   326	                        }
   327	                }
   328	
   329	                return transaction.SaveAccountSettings(ctx, store.LockingStrengthUpdate, accountID, newSettings)
   330	        })
   331	        if err != nil {
   332	                return nil, err
   333	        }
   334	
   335	        extraSettingsChanged, err := am.settingsManager.UpdateExtraSettings(ctx, accountID, userID, newSettings.Extra)
   336	        if err != nil {
   337	                return nil, err
   338	        }
   339	
   340	        am.handleRoutingPeerDNSResolutionSettings(ctx, oldSettings, newSettings, userID, accountID)
   341	        am.handleLazyConnectionSettings(ctx, oldSettings, newSettings, userID, accountID)
   342	        am.handlePeerLoginExpirationSettings(ctx, oldSettings, newSettings, userID, accountID)
   343	        am.handleGroupsPropagationSettings(ctx, oldSettings, newSettings, userID, accountID)
   344	        if err = am.handleInactivityExpirationSettings(ctx, oldSettings, newSettings, userID, accountID); err != nil {
   345	                return nil, err
   346	        }
   347	        if oldSettings.DNSDomain != newSettings.DNSDomain {
   348	                am.StoreEvent(ctx, userID, accountID, accountID, activity.AccountDNSDomainUpdated, nil)
   349	        }
   350	
   351	        if updateAccountPeers || extraSettingsChanged || groupChangesAffectPeers {
   352	                go am.UpdateAccountPeers(ctx, accountID)
   353	        }
   354	
   355	        return newSettings, nil
   356	}
   357	
   358	func (am *DefaultAccountManager) validateSettingsUpdate(ctx context.Context, transaction store.Store, newSettings, oldSettings *types.Settings, userID, accountID string) error {
   359	        halfYearLimit := 180 * 24 * time.Hour
   360	        if newSettings.PeerLoginExpiration > halfYearLimit {
   361	                return status.Errorf(status.InvalidArgument, "peer login expiration can't be larger than 180 days")
   362	        }
   363	
   364	        if newSettings.PeerLoginExpiration < time.Hour {
   365	                return status.Errorf(status.InvalidArgument, "peer login expiration can't be smaller than one hour")
   366	        }
   367	
   368	        if newSettings.DNSDomain != "" && !isDomainValid(newSettings.DNSDomain) {
   369	                return status.Errorf(status.InvalidArgument, "invalid domain \"%s\" provided for DNS domain", newSettings.DNSDomain)
   370	        }
   371	
   372	        peers, err := transaction.GetAccountPeers(ctx, store.LockingStrengthShare, accountID, "", "")
   373	        if err != nil {
   374	                return err
   375	        }
   376	
   377	        peersMap := make(map[string]*nbpeer.Peer, len(peers))
   378	        for _, peer := range peers {
   379	                peersMap[peer.ID] = peer
   380	        }
   381	
   382	        return am.integratedPeerValidator.ValidateExtraSettings(ctx, newSettings.Extra, oldSettings.Extra, peersMap, userID, accountID)
   383	}
   384	
   385	func (am *DefaultAccountManager) handleRoutingPeerDNSResolutionSettings(ctx context.Context, oldSettings, newSettings *types.Settings, userID, accountID string) {
   386	        if oldSettings.RoutingPeerDNSResolutionEnabled != newSettings.RoutingPeerDNSResolutionEnabled {
   387	                if newSettings.RoutingPeerDNSResolutionEnabled {
   388	                        am.StoreEvent(ctx, userID, accountID, accountID, activity.AccountRoutingPeerDNSResolutionEnabled, nil)
   389	                } else {
   390	                        am.StoreEvent(ctx, userID, accountID, accountID, activity.AccountRoutingPeerDNSResolutionDisabled, nil)
   391	                }
   392	        }
   393	}
   394	
   395	func (am *DefaultAccountManager) handleLazyConnectionSettings(ctx context.Context, oldSettings, newSettings *types.Settings, userID, accountID string) {
   396	        if oldSettings.LazyConnectionEnabled != newSettings.LazyConnectionEnabled {
   397	                if newSettings.LazyConnectionEnabled {
   398	                        am.StoreEvent(ctx, userID, accountID, accountID, activity.AccountLazyConnectionEnabled, nil)
   399	                } else {
   400	                        am.StoreEvent(ctx, userID, accountID, accountID, activity.AccountLazyConnectionDisabled, nil)
   401	                }
   402	        }
   403	}
   404	
   405	func (am *DefaultAccountManager) handlePeerLoginExpirationSettings(ctx context.Context, oldSettings, newSettings *types.Settings, userID, accountID string) {
   406	        if oldSettings.PeerLoginExpirationEnabled != newSettings.PeerLoginExpirationEnabled {
   407	                event := activity.AccountPeerLoginExpirationEnabled
   408	                if !newSettings.PeerLoginExpirationEnabled {
   409	                        event = activity.AccountPeerLoginExpirationDisabled
   410	                        am.peerLoginExpiry.Cancel(ctx, []string{accountID})
   411	                } else {
   412	                        am.checkAndSchedulePeerLoginExpiration(ctx, accountID)
   413	                }
   414	                am.StoreEvent(ctx, userID, accountID, accountID, event, nil)
   415	        }
   416	
   417	        if oldSettings.PeerLoginExpiration != newSettings.PeerLoginExpiration {
   418	                am.StoreEvent(ctx, userID, accountID, accountID, activity.AccountPeerLoginExpirationDurationUpdated, nil)
   419	                am.checkAndSchedulePeerLoginExpiration(ctx, accountID)
   420	        }
   421	}

    43	// AccountMeta is a struct that contains a stripped down version of the Account object.
    44	// It doesn't carry any peers, groups, policies, or routes, etc. Just some metadata (e.g. ID, created by, created at, etc).
    45	type AccountMeta struct {
    46	        // AccountId is the unique identifier of the account
    47	        AccountID      string `gorm:"column:id"`
    48	        CreatedAt      time.Time
    49	        CreatedBy      string
    50	        Domain         string
    51	        DomainCategory string
    52	}
    53	
    54	// Account represents a unique account of the system
    55	type Account struct {
    56	        // we have to name column to aid as it collides with Network.Id when work with associations
    57	        Id string `gorm:"primaryKey"`
    58	
    59	        // User.Id it was created by
    60	        CreatedBy              string
    61	        CreatedAt              time.Time
    62	        Domain                 string `gorm:"index"`
    63	        DomainCategory         string
    64	        IsDomainPrimaryAccount bool
    65	        SetupKeys              map[string]*SetupKey              `gorm:"-"`
    66	        SetupKeysG             []SetupKey                        `json:"-" gorm:"foreignKey:AccountID;references:id"`

   103	
   104	// GetRoutesToSync returns the enabled routes for the peer ID and the routes
   105	// from the ACL peers that have distribution groups associated with the peer ID.
   106	// Please mind, that the returned route.Route objects will contain Peer.Key instead of Peer.ID.
   107	func (a *Account) GetRoutesToSync(ctx context.Context, peerID string, aclPeers []*nbpeer.Peer) []*route.Route {
   108	        routes, peerDisabledRoutes := a.getRoutingPeerRoutes(ctx, peerID)
   109	        peerRoutesMembership := make(LookupMap)
   110	        for _, r := range append(routes, peerDisabledRoutes...) {
   111	                peerRoutesMembership[string(r.GetHAUniqueID())] = struct{}{}
   112	        }
   113	
   114	        groupListMap := a.GetPeerGroups(peerID)
   115	        for _, peer := range aclPeers {
   116	                activeRoutes, _ := a.getRoutingPeerRoutes(ctx, peer.ID)
   117	                groupFilteredRoutes := a.filterRoutesByGroups(activeRoutes, groupListMap)
   118	                filteredRoutes := a.filterRoutesFromPeersOfSameHAGroup(groupFilteredRoutes, peerRoutesMembership)
   119	                routes = append(routes, filteredRoutes...)
   120	        }
   121	
   122	        return routes
   123	}
   124	
   125	// filterRoutesFromPeersOfSameHAGroup filters and returns a list of routes that don't share the same HA route membership
   126	func (a *Account) filterRoutesFromPeersOfSameHAGroup(routes []*route.Route, peerMemberships LookupMap) []*route.Route {
   127	        var filteredRoutes []*route.Route
   128	        for _, r := range routes {
   129	                _, found := peerMemberships[string(r.GetHAUniqueID())]
   130	                if !found {
   131	                        filteredRoutes = append(filteredRoutes, r)
   132	                }
   133	        }
   134	        return filteredRoutes
   135	}
   136	
   137	// filterRoutesByGroups returns a list with routes that have distribution groups in the group's map
   138	func (a *Account) filterRoutesByGroups(routes []*route.Route, groupListMap LookupMap) []*route.Route {
   139	        var filteredRoutes []*route.Route
   140	        for _, r := range routes {
   141	                for _, groupID := range r.Groups {
   142	                        _, found := groupListMap[groupID]
   143	                        if found {
   144	                                filteredRoutes = append(filteredRoutes, r)
   145	                                break
   146	                        }
   147	                }
   148	        }
   149	        return filteredRoutes
   150	}
   151	
   152	// getRoutingPeerRoutes returns the enabled and disabled lists of routes that the given routing peer serves
   153	// Please mind, that the returned route.Route objects will contain Peer.Key instead of Peer.ID.
   154	// If the given is not a routing peer, then the lists are empty.
   155	func (a *Account) getRoutingPeerRoutes(ctx context.Context, peerID string) (enabledRoutes []*route.Route, disabledRoutes []*route.Route) {
   156	
   157	        peer := a.GetPeer(peerID)
   158	        if peer == nil {
   159	                log.WithContext(ctx).Errorf("peer %s that doesn't exist under account %s", peerID, a.Id)
   160	                return enabledRoutes, disabledRoutes
   161	        }
   162	
   163	        seenRoute := make(map[route.ID]struct{})
   164	
   165	        takeRoute := func(r *route.Route, id string) {
   166	                if _, ok := seenRoute[r.ID]; ok {
   167	                        return
   168	                }
   169	                seenRoute[r.ID] = struct{}{}
   170	
   171	                if r.Enabled {
   172	                        r.Peer = peer.Key
   173	                        enabledRoutes = append(enabledRoutes, r)
   174	                        return
   175	                }
   176	                disabledRoutes = append(disabledRoutes, r)
   177	        }
   178	
   179	        for _, r := range a.Routes {
   180	                for _, groupID := range r.PeerGroups {
   181	                        group := a.GetGroup(groupID)
   182	                        if group == nil {
   183	                                log.WithContext(ctx).Errorf("route %s has peers group %s that doesn't exist under account %s", r.ID, groupID, a.Id)
   184	                                continue
   185	                        }
   186	                        for _, id := range group.Peers {
   187	                                if id != peerID {
   188	                                        continue
   189	                                }
   190	
   191	                                newPeerRoute := r.Copy()
   192	                                newPeerRoute.Peer = id
   193	                                newPeerRoute.PeerGroups = nil
   194	                                newPeerRoute.ID = route.ID(string(r.ID) + ":" + id) // we have to provide unique route id when distribute network map
   195	                                takeRoute(newPeerRoute, id)
   196	                                break
   197	                        }
   198	                }
   199	                if r.Peer == peerID {
   200	                        takeRoute(r.Copy(), peerID)
   201	                }
   202	        }
   203	
   204	        return enabledRoutes, disabledRoutes
   205	}
   206	
   207	// GetRoutesByPrefixOrDomains return list of routes by account and route prefix
   208	func (a *Account) GetRoutesByPrefixOrDomains(prefix netip.Prefix, domains domain.List) []*route.Route {
   209	        var routes []*route.Route
   210	        for _, r := range a.Routes {
   211	                dynamic := r.IsDynamic()
   212	                if dynamic && r.Domains.PunycodeString() == domains.PunycodeString() ||
   213	                        !dynamic && r.Network.String() == prefix.String() {
   214	                        routes = append(routes, r)
   215	                }
   216	        }
   217	
   218	        return routes
   219	}
   220	
   221	// GetGroup returns a group by ID if exists, nil otherwise
   222	func (a *Account) GetGroup(groupID string) *Group {
   223	        return a.Groups[groupID]
   224	}
   225	
   226	// GetPeerNetworkMap returns the networkmap for the given peer ID.
   227	func (a *Account) GetPeerNetworkMap(
   228	        ctx context.Context,
   229	        peerID string,
   230	        peersCustomZone nbdns.CustomZone,
   231	        validatedPeersMap map[string]struct{},
   232	        resourcePolicies map[string][]*Policy,
   233	        routers map[string]map[string]*routerTypes.NetworkRouter,
   234	        metrics *telemetry.AccountManagerMetrics,
   235	) *NetworkMap {
   236	        start := time.Now()
   237	
   238	        peer := a.Peers[peerID]
   239	        if peer == nil {
   240	                return &NetworkMap{
   241	                        Network: a.Network.Copy(),
   242	                }
   243	        }
   244	
   245	        if _, ok := validatedPeersMap[peerID]; !ok {
   246	                return &NetworkMap{
   247	                        Network: a.Network.Copy(),
   248	                }
   249	        }
   250	
   251	        aclPeers, firewallRules := a.GetPeerConnectionResources(ctx, peerID, validatedPeersMap)
   252	        // exclude expired peers
   253	        var peersToConnect []*nbpeer.Peer
   254	        var expiredPeers []*nbpeer.Peer
   255	        for _, p := range aclPeers {
   256	                expired, _ := p.LoginExpired(a.Settings.PeerLoginExpiration)
   257	                if a.Settings.PeerLoginExpirationEnabled && expired {
   258	                        expiredPeers = append(expiredPeers, p)
   259	                        continue
   260	                }
   261	                peersToConnect = append(peersToConnect, p)
   262	        }
   263	
   264	        routesUpdate := a.GetRoutesToSync(ctx, peerID, peersToConnect)
   265	        routesFirewallRules := a.GetPeerRoutesFirewallRules(ctx, peerID, validatedPeersMap)
   266	        isRouter, networkResourcesRoutes, sourcePeers := a.GetNetworkResourcesRoutesToSync(ctx, peerID, resourcePolicies, routers)
   267	        var networkResourcesFirewallRules []*RouteFirewallRule
   268	        if isRouter {
   269	                networkResourcesFirewallRules = a.GetPeerNetworkResourceFirewallRules(ctx, peer, validatedPeersMap, networkResourcesRoutes, resourcePolicies)
   270	        }
   271	        peersToConnectIncludingRouters := a.addNetworksRoutingPeers(networkResourcesRoutes, peer, peersToConnect, expiredPeers, isRouter, sourcePeers)
   272	
   273	        dnsManagementStatus := a.getPeerDNSManagementStatus(peerID)
   274	        dnsUpdate := nbdns.Config{
   275	                ServiceEnable: dnsManagementStatus,
   276	        }
   277	
   278	        if dnsManagementStatus {
   279	                var zones []nbdns.CustomZone
   280	
   281	                if peersCustomZone.Domain != "" {
   282	                        zones = append(zones, peersCustomZone)
   283	                }
   284	                dnsUpdate.CustomZones = zones
   285	                dnsUpdate.NameServerGroups = getPeerNSGroups(a, peerID)
   286	        }
   287	
   288	        nm := &NetworkMap{
   289	                Peers:               peersToConnectIncludingRouters,
   290	                Network:             a.Network.Copy(),
   291	                Routes:              slices.Concat(networkResourcesRoutes, routesUpdate),
   292	                DNSConfig:           dnsUpdate,
   293	                OfflinePeers:        expiredPeers,
   294	                FirewallRules:       firewallRules,
   295	                RoutesFirewallRules: slices.Concat(networkResourcesFirewallRules, routesFirewallRules),
   296	        }
   297	
   298	        if metrics != nil {
   299	                objectCount := int64(len(peersToConnectIncludingRouters) + len(expiredPeers) + len(routesUpdate) + len(networkResourcesRoutes) + len(firewallRules) + +len(networkResourcesFirewallRules) + len(routesFirewallRules))
   300	                metrics.CountNetworkMapObjects(objectCount)
   301	                metrics.CountGetPeerNetworkMapDuration(time.Since(start))
   302	
   303	                if objectCount > 5000 {
   304	                        log.WithContext(ctx).Tracef("account: %s has a total resource count of %d objects, "+
   305	                                "peers to connect: %d, expired peers: %d, routes: %d, firewall rules: %d, network resources routes: %d, network resources firewall rules: %d, routes firewall rules: %d",
   306	                                a.Id, objectCount, len(peersToConnectIncludingRouters), len(expiredPeers), len(routesUpdate), len(firewallRules), len(networkResourcesRoutes), len(networkResourcesFirewallRules), len(routesFirewallRules))
   307	                }
   308	        }
   309	
   310	        return nm
   311	}
   312	
   313	func (a *Account) addNetworksRoutingPeers(
   314	        networkResourcesRoutes []*route.Route,
   315	        peer *nbpeer.Peer,
   316	        peersToConnect []*nbpeer.Peer,
   317	        expiredPeers []*nbpeer.Peer,
   318	        isRouter bool,
   319	        sourcePeers map[string]struct{},
   320	) []*nbpeer.Peer {
   321	
   322	        networkRoutesPeers := make(map[string]struct{}, len(networkResourcesRoutes))
   323	        for _, r := range networkResourcesRoutes {
   324	                networkRoutesPeers[r.PeerID] = struct{}{}
   325	        }
   326	
   327	        delete(sourcePeers, peer.ID)
   328	        delete(networkRoutesPeers, peer.ID)
   329	
   330	        for _, existingPeer := range peersToConnect {
   331	                delete(sourcePeers, existingPeer.ID)
   332	                delete(networkRoutesPeers, existingPeer.ID)
   333	        }
   334	        for _, expPeer := range expiredPeers {
   335	                delete(sourcePeers, expPeer.ID)
   336	                delete(networkRoutesPeers, expPeer.ID)
   337	        }
   338	
   339	        missingPeers := make(map[string]struct{}, len(sourcePeers)+len(networkRoutesPeers))
   340	        if isRouter {
   341	                for p := range sourcePeers {
   342	                        missingPeers[p] = struct{}{}
   343	                }
   344	        }
   345	        for p := range networkRoutesPeers {
   346	                missingPeers[p] = struct{}{}
   347	        }
   348	
   349	        for p := range missingPeers {
   350	                if missingPeer := a.Peers[p]; missingPeer != nil {
   351	                        peersToConnect = append(peersToConnect, missingPeer)
   352	                }
   353	        }
   354	
   355	        return peersToConnect
   356	}
   357	
   358	func getPeerNSGroups(account *Account, peerID string) []*nbdns.NameServerGroup {
   359	        groupList := account.GetPeerGroups(peerID)
   360	
   361	        var peerNSGroups []*nbdns.NameServerGroup
   362	
   363	        for _, nsGroup := range account.NameServerGroups {
   364	                if !nsGroup.Enabled {
   365	                        continue
   366	                }
   367	                for _, gID := range nsGroup.Groups {
   368	                        _, found := groupList[gID]
   369	                        if found {
   370	                                if !peerIsNameserver(account.GetPeer(peerID), nsGroup) {
   371	                                        peerNSGroups = append(peerNSGroups, nsGroup.Copy())
   372	                                        break
   373	                                }
   374	                        }
   375	                }
   376	        }
   377	
   378	        return peerNSGroups
   379	}
   380	
   381	// peerIsNameserver returns true if the peer is a nameserver for a nsGroup
   382	func peerIsNameserver(peer *nbpeer.Peer, nsGroup *nbdns.NameServerGroup) bool {
   383	        for _, ns := range nsGroup.NameServers {
   384	                if peer.IP.Equal(ns.IP.AsSlice()) {
   385	                        return true
   386	                }
   387	        }
   388	        return false
   389	}
   390	
   391	func AddPeerLabelsToAccount(ctx context.Context, account *Account, peerLabels LookupMap) {
   392	        for _, peer := range account.Peers {
   393	                label, err := GetPeerHostLabel(peer.Name, peerLabels)
   394	                if err != nil {
   395	                        log.WithContext(ctx).Errorf("got an error while generating a peer host label. Peer name %s, error: %v. Trying with the peer's meta hostname", peer.Name, err)
   396	                        label, err = GetPeerHostLabel(peer.Meta.Hostname, peerLabels)
   397	                        if err != nil {
   398	                                log.WithContext(ctx).Errorf("got another error while generating a peer host label with hostname. Peer hostname %s, error: %v. Skipping", peer.Meta.Hostname, err)
   399	                                continue
   400	                        }
   401	                }
   402	                peer.DNSLabel = label
   403	                peerLabels[label] = struct{}{}
   404	        }
   405	}
   406	
   407	func GetPeerHostLabel(name string, peerLabels LookupMap) (string, error) {
   408	        label, err := nbdns.GetParsedDomainLabel(name)
   409	        if err != nil {
   410	                return "", err
   411	        }
   412	
   413	        uniqueLabel := getUniqueHostLabel(label, peerLabels)
   414	        if uniqueLabel == "" {
   415	                return "", fmt.Errorf("couldn't find a unique valid label for %s, parsed label %s", name, label)
   416	        }
   417	        return uniqueLabel, nil
   418	}
   419	
   420	// getUniqueHostLabel look for a unique host label, and if doesn't find add a suffix up to 999
   421	func getUniqueHostLabel(name string, peerLabels LookupMap) string {
   422	        _, found := peerLabels[name]
   423	        if !found {
   424	                return name
   425	        }
   426	        for i := 1; i < 1000; i++ {
   427	                nameWithSuffix := name + "-" + strconv.Itoa(i)
   428	                _, found = peerLabels[nameWithSuffix]
   429	                if !found {
   430	                        return nameWithSuffix
   431	                }
   432	        }
   433	        return ""
   434	}
   435	
   436	func (a *Account) GetPeersCustomZone(ctx context.Context, dnsDomain string) nbdns.CustomZone {
   437	        var merr *multierror.Error
   438	
   439	        if dnsDomain == "" {
   440	                log.WithContext(ctx).Error("no dns domain is set, returning empty zone")
   441	                return nbdns.CustomZone{}
   442	        }
   443	
   444	        customZone := nbdns.CustomZone{
   445	                Domain:  dns.Fqdn(dnsDomain),
   446	                Records: make([]nbdns.SimpleRecord, 0, len(a.Peers)),
   447	        }
   448	
   449	        domainSuffix := "." + dnsDomain
   450	
   451	        var sb strings.Builder
   452	        for _, peer := range a.Peers {
   453	                if peer.DNSLabel == "" {
   454	                        merr = multierror.Append(merr, fmt.Errorf("peer %s has an empty DNS label", peer.Name))
   455	                        continue
   456	                }
   457	
   458	                sb.Grow(len(peer.DNSLabel) + len(domainSuffix))
   459	                sb.WriteString(peer.DNSLabel)
   460	                sb.WriteString(domainSuffix)
   461	
   462	                customZone.Records = append(customZone.Records, nbdns.SimpleRecord{
   463	                        Name:<response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. You should retry this tool after you have searched inside the file with `grep -n` in order to find the line numbers of what you are looking for.</NOTE>

   156	// BuildManager creates a new DefaultAccountManager with a provided Store
   157	func BuildManager(
   158	        ctx context.Context,
   159	        store store.Store,
   160	        peersUpdateManager *PeersUpdateManager,
   161	        idpManager idp.Manager,
   162	        singleAccountModeDomain string,
   163	        dnsDomain string,
   164	        eventStore activity.Store,
   165	        geo geolocation.Geolocation,
   166	        userDeleteFromIDPEnabled bool,
   167	        integratedPeerValidator integrated_validator.IntegratedValidator,
   168	        metrics telemetry.AppMetrics,
   169	        proxyController port_forwarding.Controller,
   170	        settingsManager settings.Manager,
   171	        permissionsManager permissions.Manager,
   172	) (*DefaultAccountManager, error) {
   173	        start := time.Now()
   174	        defer func() {
   175	                log.WithContext(ctx).Debugf("took %v to instantiate account manager", time.Since(start))
   176	        }()
   177	
   178	        am := &DefaultAccountManager{
   179	                Store:                    store,
   180	                geo:                      geo,
   181	                peersUpdateManager:       peersUpdateManager,
   182	                idpManager:               idpManager,
   183	                ctx:                      context.Background(),
   184	                cacheMux:                 sync.Mutex{},
   185	                cacheLoading:             map[string]chan struct{}{},
   186	                dnsDomain:                dnsDomain,
   187	                eventStore:               eventStore,
   188	                peerLoginExpiry:          NewDefaultScheduler(),
   189	                peerInactivityExpiry:     NewDefaultScheduler(),
   190	                userDeleteFromIDPEnabled: userDeleteFromIDPEnabled,
   191	                integratedPeerValidator:  integratedPeerValidator,
   192	                metrics:                  metrics,
   193	                requestBuffer:            NewAccountRequestBuffer(ctx, store),
   194	                proxyController:          proxyController,
   195	                settingsManager:          settingsManager,
   196	                permissionsManager:       permissionsManager,
   197	        }
   198	
   199	        am.startWarmup(ctx)
   200	
   201	        accountsCounter, err := store.GetAccountsCounter(ctx)
   202	        if err != nil {
   203	                log.WithContext(ctx).Error(err)
   204	        }
   205	
   206	        // enable single account mode only if configured by user and number of existing accounts is not grater than 1
   207	        am.singleAccountMode = singleAccountModeDomain != "" && accountsCounter <= 1
   208	        if am.singleAccountMode {
   209	                if !isDomainValid(singleAccountModeDomain) {
   210	                        return nil, status.Errorf(status.InvalidArgument, "invalid domain \"%s\" provided for a single account mode. Please review your input for --single-account-mode-domain", singleAccountModeDomain)
   211	                }
   212	                am.singleAccountModeDomain = singleAccountModeDomain
   213	                log.WithContext(ctx).Infof("single account mode enabled, accounts number %d", accountsCounter)
   214	        } else {
   215	                log.WithContext(ctx).Infof("single account mode disabled, accounts number %d", accountsCounter)
   216	        }
   217	
   218	        cacheStore, err := nbcache.NewStore(ctx, nbcache.DefaultIDPCacheExpirationMax, nbcache.DefaultIDPCacheCleanupInterval)
   219	        if err != nil {
   220	                return nil, fmt.Errorf("getting cache store: %s", err)
   221	        }
   222	        am.externalCacheManager = nbcache.NewUserDataCache(cacheStore)
   223	        am.cacheManager = nbcache.NewAccountUserDataCache(am.loadAccount, cacheStore)
   224	
   225	        if !isNil(am.idpManager) {
   226	                go func() {
   227	                        err := am.warmupIDPCache(ctx, cacheStore)
   228	                        if err != nil {
   229	                                log.WithContext(ctx).Warnf("failed warming up cache due to error: %v", err)
   230	                                // todo retry?
   231	                                return
   232	                        }
   233	                }()
   234	        }
   235	
   236	        am.integratedPeerValidator.SetPeerInvalidationListener(func(accountID string) {
   237	                am.onPeersInvalidated(ctx, accountID)
   238	        })
   239	
   240	        return am, nil

    84	// SetupCloseHandler handles SIGTERM signal and exits with success
    85	func SetupCloseHandler() {
    86	        c := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
    87	        signal.Notify(c, os.Interrupt)
    88	        go func() {
    89	                for range c {
    90	                        fmt.Println("\r- Ctrl+C pressed in Terminal")
    91	                        stopCh <- 0
    92	                }
    93	        }()
    94	}

    94	                        //nolint

    97	                        err := util.InitLog(logLevel, logFile)
    98	                        if err != nil {
    99	                                return fmt.Errorf("failed initializing log %v", err)
   100	                        }
   101	
   102	                        // detect whether user specified a port
   103	                        userPort := cmd.Flag("port").Changed
   104	
   105	                        config, err = loadMgmtConfig(ctx, types.MgmtConfigPath)
   106	                        if err != nil {
   107	                                return fmt.Errorf("failed reading provided config file: %s: %v", types.MgmtConfigPath, err)
   108	                        }
   109	
   110	                        if cmd.Flag(idpSignKeyRefreshEnabledFlagName).Changed {
   111	                                config.HttpConfig.IdpSignKeyRefreshEnabled = idpSignKeyRefreshEnabled
   112	                        }
   113	
   114	                        tlsEnabled := false
   115	                        if mgmtLetsencryptDomain != "" || (config.HttpConfig.CertFile != "" && config.HttpConfig.CertKey != "") {
   116	                                tlsEnabled = true
   117	                        }
   118	
   119	                        if !userPort {
   120	                                // different defaults for port when tls enabled/disabled
   121	                                if tlsEnabled {
   122	                                        mgmtPort = 443
   123	                                } else {
   124	                                        mgmtPort = 80
   125	                                }
   126	                        }
   127

   129	                        if !valid || len(dnsDomain) > 192 {
   130	                                return fmt.Errorf("failed parsing the provided dns-domain. Valid status: %t, Length: %d", valid, len(dnsDomain))
   131	                        }
   132	
   133	                        return nil
   134	                },

   136	                        flag.Parse()
   137	
   138	                        ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(cmd.Context())
   139	                        defer cancel()
   140	                        //nolint
   141	                        ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, hook.ExecutionContextKey, hook.SystemSource)
   142	
   143	                        err := handleRebrand(cmd)
   144	                        if err != nil {
   145	                                return fmt.Errorf("failed to migrate files %v", err)
   146	                        }
   147	
   148	                        if _, err = os.Stat(config.Datadir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
   149	                                err = os.MkdirAll(config.Datadir, 0755)
   150	                                if err != nil {
   151	                                        return fmt.Errorf("failed creating datadir: %s: %v", config.Datadir, err)
   152	                                }
   153	                        }
   154	                        appMetrics, err := telemetry.NewDefaultAppMetrics(cmd.Context())
   155	                        if err != nil {
   156	                                return err
   157	                        }
   158	                        err = appMetrics.Expose(ctx, mgmtMetricsPort, "/metrics")
   159	                        if err != nil {
   160	                                return err
   161	                        }
   162	
   163	                        integrationMetrics, err := integrations.InitIntegrationMetrics(ctx, appMetrics)
   164	                        if err != nil {
   165	                                return err
   166	                        }
   167	
   168	                        store, err := store.NewStore(ctx, config.StoreConfig.Engine, config.Datadir, appMetrics, false)
   169	                        if err != nil {
   170	                                return fmt.Errorf("failed creating Store: %s: %v", config.Datadir, err)
   171	                        }
   172	                        peersUpdateManager := server.NewPeersUpdateManager(appMetrics)
   173	
   174	                        var idpManager idp.Manager
   175	                        if config.IdpManagerConfig != nil {
   176	                                idpManager, err = idp.NewManager(ctx, *config.IdpManagerConfig, appMetrics)
   177	                                if err != nil {
   178	                                        return fmt.Errorf("failed retrieving a new idp manager with err: %v", err)
   179	                                }
   180	                        }
   181	
   182	                        if disableSingleAccMode {
   183	                                mgmtSingleAccModeDomain = ""
   184	                        }
   185	                        eventStore, key, err := integrations.InitEventStore(ctx, config.Datadir, config.DataStoreEncryptionKey, integrationMetrics)
   186	                        if err != nil {
   187	                                return fmt.Errorf("failed to initialize database: %s", err)
   188	                        }
   189	
   190	                        if config.DataStoreEncryptionKey != key {
   191	                                log.WithContext(ctx).Infof("update config with activity store key")
   192	                                config.DataStoreEncryptionKey = key
   193	                                err := updateMgmtConfig(ctx, types.MgmtConfigPath, config)
   194	                                if err != nil {
   195	                                        return fmt.Errorf("failed to write out store encryption key: %s", err)
   196	                                }
   197	                        }
   198	
   199	                        geo, err := geolocation.NewGeolocation(ctx, config.Datadir, !disableGeoliteUpdate)
   200	                        if err != nil {
   201	                                log.WithContext(ctx).Warnf("could not initialize geolocation service. proceeding without geolocation support: %v", err)
   202	                        } else {
   203	                                log.WithContext(ctx).Infof("geolocation service has been initialized from %s", config.Datadir)
   204	                        }
   205	
   206	                        integratedPeerValidator, err := integrations.NewIntegratedValidator(ctx, eventStore)
   207	                        if err != nil {
   208	                                return fmt.Errorf("failed to initialize integrated peer validator: %v", err)
   209	                        }
   210	
   211	                        permissionsManager := integrations.InitPermissionsManager(store)
   212	                        userManager := users.NewManager(store)
   213	                        extraSettingsManager := integrations.NewManager(eventStore)
   214	                        settingsManager := settings.NewManager(store, userManager, extraSettingsManager, permissionsManager)
   215	                        peersManager := peers.NewManager(store, permissionsManager)
   216	                        proxyController := integrations.NewController(store)
   217	                        accountManager, err := server.BuildManager(ctx, store, peersUpdateManager, idpManager, mgmtSingleAccModeDomain,
   218	                                dnsDomain, eventStore, geo, userDeleteFromIDPEnabled, integratedPeerValidator, appMetrics, proxyController, settingsManager, permissionsManager)
   219	                        if err != nil {
   220	                                return fmt.Errorf("failed to build default manager: %v", err)
   221	                        }
   222	
   223	                        secretsManager := server.NewTimeBasedAuthSecretsManager(peersUpdateManager, config.TURNConfig, config.Relay, settingsManager)
   224	
   225	                        trustedPeers := config.ReverseProxy.TrustedPeers
   226	                        defaultTrustedPeers := []netip.Prefix{netip.MustParsePrefix("0.0.0.0/0"), netip.MustParsePrefix("::/0")}
   227	                        if len(trustedPeers) == 0 || slices.Equal[[]netip.Prefix](trustedPeers, defaultTrustedPeers) {
   228	                                log.WithContext(ctx).Warn("TrustedPeers are configured to default value '0.0.0.0/0', '::/0'. This allows connection IP spoofing.")
   229	                                trustedPeers = defaultTrustedPeers
   230	                        }
   231	                        trustedHTTPProxies := config.ReverseProxy.TrustedHTTPProxies
   232	                        trustedProxiesCount := config.ReverseProxy.TrustedHTTPProxiesCount
   233	                        if len(trustedHTTPProxies) > 0 && trustedProxiesCount > 0 {
   234	                                log.WithContext(ctx).Warn("TrustedHTTPProxies and TrustedHTTPProxiesCount both are configured. " +
   235	                                        "This is not recommended way to extract X-Forwarded-For. Consider using one of these options.")
   236	                        }
   237	                        realipOpts := []realip.Option{
   238	                                realip.WithTrustedPeers(trustedPeers),
   239	                                realip.WithTrustedProxies(trustedHTTPProxies),
   240	                                realip.WithTrustedProxiesCount(trustedProxiesCount),
   241	                                realip.WithHeaders([]string{realip.XForwardedFor, realip.XRealIp}),
   242	                        }
   243	                        gRPCOpts := []grpc.ServerOption{
   244	                                grpc.KeepaliveEnforcementPolicy(kaep),
   245	                                grpc.KeepaliveParams(kasp),
   246	                                grpc.ChainUnaryInterceptor(realip.UnaryServerInterceptorOpts(realipOpts...), unaryInterceptor),
   247	                                grpc.ChainStreamInterceptor(realip.StreamServerInterceptorOpts(realipOpts...), streamInterceptor),
   248	                        }
   249	
   250	                        var certManager *autocert.Manager
   251	                        var tlsConfig *tls.Config
   252	                        tlsEnabled := false
   253	                        if config.HttpConfig.LetsEncryptDomain != "" {
   254	                                certManager, err = encryption.CreateCertManager(config.Datadir, config.HttpConfig.LetsEncryptDomain)
   255	                                if err != nil {
   256	                                        return fmt.Errorf("failed creating LetsEncrypt cert manager: %v", err)
   257	                                }
   258	                                transportCredentials := credentials.NewTLS(certManager.TLSConfig())
   259	                                gRPCOpts = append(gRPCOpts, grpc.Creds(transportCredentials))
   260	                                tlsEnabled = true
   261	                        } else if config.HttpConfig.CertFile != "" && config.HttpConfig.CertKey != "" {
   262	                                tlsConfig, err = loadTLSConfig(config.HttpConfig.CertFile, config.HttpConfig.CertKey)
   263	                                if err != nil {
   264	                                        log.WithContext(ctx).Errorf("cannot load TLS credentials: %v", err)
   265	                                        return err
   266	                                }
   267	                                transportCredentials := credentials.NewTLS(tlsConfig)
   268	                                gRPCOpts = append(gRPCOpts, grpc.Creds(transportCredentials))
   269	                                tlsEnabled = true
   270	                        }
   271	
   272	                        authManager := auth.NewManager(store,
   273	                                config.HttpConfig.AuthIssuer,
   274	                                config.HttpConfig.AuthAudience,
   275	                                config.HttpConfig.AuthKeysLocation,
   276	                                config.HttpConfig.AuthUserIDClaim,
   277	                                config.GetAuthAudiences(),
   278	                                config.HttpConfig.IdpSignKeyRefreshEnabled)
   279	
   280	                        groupsManager := groups.NewManager(store, permissionsManager, accountManager)
   281	                        resourcesManager := resources.NewManager(store, permissionsManager, groupsManager, accountManager)
   282	                        routersManager := routers.NewManager(store, permissionsManager, accountManager)
   283	                        networksManager := networks.NewManager(store, permissionsManager, resourcesManager, routersManager, accountManager)
   284	
   285	                        httpAPIHandler, err := nbhttp.NewAPIHandler(ctx, accountManager, networksManager, resourcesManager, routersManager, groupsManager, geo, authManager, appMetrics, integratedPeerValidator, proxyController, permissionsManager, peersManager, settingsManager)
   286	
   287	                        if err != nil {
   288	                                return fmt.Errorf("failed creating HTTP API handler: %v", err)
   289	                        }
   290	
   291	                        ephemeralManager := server.NewEphemeralManager(store, accountManager)
   292	                        ephemeralManager.LoadInitialPeers(ctx)
   293	
   294	                        gRPCAPIHandler := grpc.NewServer(gRPCOpts...)
   295	                        srv, err := server.NewServer(ctx, config, accountManager, settingsManager, peersUpdateManager, secretsManager, appMetrics, ephemeralManager, authManager)
   296	                        if err != nil {
   297	                                return fmt.Errorf("failed creating gRPC API handler: %v", err)
   298	                        }
   299	                        mgmtProto.RegisterManagementServiceServer(gRPCAPIHandler, srv)
   300	
   301	                        installationID, err := getInstallationID(ctx, store)
   302	                        if err != nil {
   303	                                log.WithContext(ctx).Errorf("cannot load TLS credentials: %v", err)
   304	                                return err
   305	                        }
   306	
   307	                        if !disableMetrics {
   308	                                idpManager := "disabled"
   309	                                if config.IdpManagerConfig != nil && config.IdpManagerConfig.ManagerType != "" {
   310	                                        idpManager = config.IdpManagerConfig.ManagerType
   311	                                }
   312	                                metricsWorker := metrics.NewWorker(ctx, installationID, store, peersUpdateManager, idpManager)
   313	                                go metricsWorker.Run(ctx)
   314	                        }
   315	
   316	                        var compatListener net.Listener
   317	                        if mgmtPort != ManagementLegacyPort {
   318	                                // The Management gRPC server was running on port 33073 previously. Old agents that are already connected to it
   319	                                // are using port 33073. For compatibility purposes we keep running a 2nd gRPC server on port 33073.
   320	                                compatListener, err = serveGRPC(ctx, gRPCAPIHandler, ManagementLegacyPort)
   321	                                if err != nil {
   322	                                        return err
   323	                                }
   324	                                log.WithContext(ctx).Infof("running gRPC backward compatibility server: %s", compatListener.Addr().String())
   325	                        }
   326	
   327	                        rootHandler := handlerFunc(gRPCAPIHandler, httpAPIHandler)
   328	                        var listener net.Listener
   329	                        if certManager != nil {
   330	                                // a call to certManager.Listener() always creates a new listener so we do it once
   331	                                cml := certManager.Listener()
   332	                                if mgmtPort == 443 {
   333	                                        // CertManager, HTTP and gRPC API all on the same port
   334	                                        rootHandler = certManager.HTTPHandler(rootHandler)
   335	                                        listener = cml
   336	                                } else {
   337	                                        listener, err = tls.Listen("tcp", fmt.Sprintf(":%d", mgmtPort), certManager.TLSConfig())
   338	                                        if err != nil {
   339	                                                return fmt.Errorf("failed creating TLS listener on port %d: %v", mgmtPort, err)
   340	                                        }
   341	                                        log.WithContext(ctx).Infof("running HTTP server (LetsEncrypt challenge handler): %s", cml.Addr().String())
   342	                                        serveHTTP(ctx, cml, certManager.HTTPHandler(nil))
   343	                                }
   344	                        } else if tlsConfig != nil {
   345	                                listener, err = tls.Listen("tcp", fmt.Sprintf(":%d", mgmtPort), tlsConfig)
   346	                                if err != nil {
   347	                                        return fmt.Errorf("failed creating TLS listener on port %d: %v", mgmtPort, err)
   348	                                }
   349	                        } else {
   350	                                listener, err = net.Listen("tcp", fmt.Sprintf(":%d", mgmtPort))
   351	                                if err != nil {
   352	                                        return fmt.Errorf("failed creating TCP listener on port %d: %v", mgmtPort, err)
   353	                                }
   354	                        }
   355	
   356	                        log.WithContext(ctx).Infof("management server version %s", version.NetbirdVersion())
   357	                        log.WithContext(ctx).Infof("running HTTP server and gRPC server on the same port: %s", listener.Addr().String())
   358	                        serveGRPCWithHTTP(ctx, listener, rootHandler, tlsEnabled)
   359	
   360	                        SetupCloseHandler()
   361	
   362	                        <-stopCh
   363	                        integratedPeerValidator.Stop(ctx)
   364	                        if geo != nil {
   365	                                _ = geo.Stop()
   366	                        }
   367	                        ephemeralManager.Stop()
   368	                        _ = appMetrics.Close()
   369	                        _ = listener.Close()
   370	                        if certManager != nil {
   371	                                _ = certManager.Listener().Close()
   372	                        }
   373	                        gRPCAPIHandler.Stop()
   374	                        _ = store.Close(ctx)
   375	                        _ = eventStore.Close(ctx)
   376	                        log.WithContext(ctx).Infof("stopped Management Service")
   377	
   378	                        return nil
   379	                },
   380	        }
   381	)
   382	
   383	func unaryInterceptor(
   384	        ctx context.Context,
   385	        req interface{},
   386	        info *grpc.UnaryServerInfo,
   387	        handler grpc.UnaryHandler,
   388	) (interface{}, error) {
   389	        reqID := uuid.New().String()
   390	        //nolint
   391	        ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, hook.ExecutionContextKey, hook.GRPCSource)
   392	        //nolint
   393	        ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, nbContext.RequestIDKey, reqID)
   394	        return handler(ctx, req)
   395	}
   396	
   397	func streamInterceptor(
   398	        srv interface{},
   399	        ss grpc.ServerStream,
   400	        info *grpc.StreamServerInfo,
   401	        handler grpc.StreamHandler,
   402	) error {
   403	        reqID := uuid.New().String()
   404	        wrapped := grpcMiddleware.WrapServerStream(ss)
   405	        //nolint
   406	        ctx := context.WithValue(ss.Context(), hook.ExecutionContextKey, hook.GRPCSource)
   407	        //nolint
   408	        wrapped.WrappedContext = context.WithValue(ctx, nbContext.RequestIDKey, reqID)
   409	        return handler(srv, wrapped)
   410	}
   411	
   412	func notifyStop(ctx context.Context, msg string) {
   413	        select {
   414	        case stopCh <- 1:
   415	                log.WithContext(ctx).Error(msg)
   416	        default:
   417	                // stop has been already called, nothing to report
   418	        }
   419	}
   420	
   421	func getInstallationID(ctx context.Context, store store.Store) (string, error) {
   422	        installationID := store.GetInstallationID()
   423	        if installationID != "" {
   424	                return installationID, nil
   425	        }
   426	
   427	        installationID = strings.ToUpper(uuid.New().String())
   428	        err := store.SaveInstallationID(ctx, installationID)
   429	        if err != nil {
   430	                return "", err
   431	        }
   432	        return installationID, nil
   433	}
   434	
   435	func serveGRPC(ctx context.Context, grpcServer *grpc.Server, port int) (net.Listener, error) {
   436	        listener, err := net.Listen("tcp", fmt.Sprintf(":%d", port))
   437	        if err != nil {
   438	                return nil, err
   439	        }
   440	        go func() {
   441	                err := grpcServer.Serve(listener)
   442	                if err != nil {
   443	                        notifyStop(ctx, fmt.Sprintf("failed running gRPC server on port %d: %v", port, err))
   444	                }
   445	        }()
   446	        return listener, nil
   447	}
   448

    58	// GetAuthAudiences returns the audience from the http config and device authorization flow config
    59	func (c Config) GetAuthAudiences() []string {
    60	        audiences := []string{c.HttpConfig.AuthAudience}
    61	
    62	        if c.HttpConfig.ExtraAuthAudience != "" {
    63	                audiences = append(audiences, c.HttpConfig.ExtraAuthAudience)
    64	        }
    65	
    66	        if c.DeviceAuthorizationFlow != nil && c.DeviceAuthorizationFlow.ProviderConfig.Audience != "" {
    67	                audiences = append(audiences, c.DeviceAuthorizationFlow.ProviderConfig.Audience)
    68	        }
    69	
    70	        return audiences
    71	}
    72	
    73	// TURNConfig is a config of the TURNCredentialsManager
    74	type TURNConfig struct {
    75	        TimeBasedCredentials bool
    76	        CredentialsTTL       util.Duration
    77	        Secret               string
    78	        Turns                []*Host
    79	}

    88	// HttpServerConfig is a config of the HTTP Management service server
    89	type HttpServerConfig struct {
    90	        LetsEncryptDomain string
    91	        // CertFile is the location of the certificate
    92	        CertFile string
    93	        // CertKey is the location of the certificate private key
    94	        CertKey string
    95	        // AuthAudience identifies the recipients that the JWT is intended for (aud in JWT)
    96	        AuthAudience string
    97	        // AuthIssuer identifies principal that issued the JWT
    98	        AuthIssuer string
    99	        // AuthUserIDClaim is the name of the claim that used as user ID
   100	        AuthUserIDClaim string
   101	        // AuthKeysLocation is a location of JWT key set containing the public keys used to verify JWT
   102	        AuthKeysLocation string
   103	        // OIDCConfigEndpoint is the endpoint of an IDP manager to get OIDC configuration
   104	        OIDCConfigEndpoint string
   105	        // IdpSignKeyRefreshEnabled identifies the signing key is currently being rotated or not
   106	        IdpSignKeyRefreshEnabled bool
   107	        // Extra audience
   108	        ExtraAuthAudience string
   109	}

   111	// Host represents a Netbird host (e.g. STUN, TURN, Signal)
   112	type Host struct {
   113	        Proto Protocol
   114	        // URI e.g. turns://stun.netbird.io:4430 or signal.netbird.io:10000
   115	        URI      string
   116	        Username string
   117	        Password string
   118	}
